title: Functions
summary: A function is a reusable block of code.
You define it once and call it many times.
It can take inputs called parameters.
It can give back a result with return.

sections.text: When you find yourself writing the same steps again and again, it is time for a function. A function bundles steps under one name so you can run them whenever you want, just by calling the name.

sections.title: Defining and Calling
sections.text: You define a function by giving it a name and a block of code to run. Then you call it by writing its name with parentheses. Defining is like writing the instructions. Calling is like pressing the button that runs them.

sections.title: Parameters and Return
sections.text: A function can take inputs, called parameters, so it can work on different values each time. It can also hand back a result using the word return. Give it numbers, get back their sum. Inputs go in, a result comes out.

media.alt: Inputs going into a function and a result coming out

sections.title: A Coffee Machine
sections.text: A function is like a coffee machine. You built it once. Each time you want coffee, you press the button, that is the call. You can put in different inputs, like water and beans, those are parameters. And it hands you a finished cup, that is the return value.

media.alt: A coffee machine taking inputs and handing back a finished cup

sections.title: Using a Function
items: Define the function with a clear name.
List any parameters it needs in the parentheses.
Write the code that should run inside it.
Use return to send back a result if needed.
Call the function by name whenever you want it to run.

sections.text: Functions can be stored in variables and even passed to other functions, just like any other value. This idea is at the heart of a lot of modern JavaScript.

terms.definition: A reusable block of code you can call.
Running a function by its name.
Sends a result back from a function.
Able to be used again and again.

options: A reusable block of code you can call
questions.explanation: A function is a reusable block of code you define once and call many times.
Parameters are the inputs a function takes so it can work on different values.

options: Hands back a result from the function
questions.explanation: return hands a result back out of the function.
